# GlassKit UI

> The open-source React component library for Meta Ray-Ban Display (smart-glasses) web apps. It ships the platform primitives Meta does not: a glasses-tuned D-pad focus engine, the Neural Band gesture hooks, and additive-light components for the 600×600 see-through lens.

The lens is an *additive* display: pure black (`#000`) emits no light and shows the real world through it. So components are built from emitted light — luminous hairline edges, a single phosphor-green accent (`#36e27f`), and soft glow — never fills or backdrop-blur. Design rules: one task per view, 3–5 focusable elements max, tabular numerals, RTL-safe logical CSS, and never mirror world-anchored components.

## SDK (`@glasskit/glasses-ui`)

- `GlassViewport` — the 600×600 lens surface; wrap your app in it.
- `useDpad()` — arrow keys / Neural Band move a focus ring between `.focusable` elements; Enter activates.
- `useDeviceOrientation()`, `useDeviceMotion()`, `useGeolocation()`, `useNeuralBand()` — W3C sensor + gesture hooks.
- `styles.css` — the additive-lens design system, scoped under `.glass-viewport`.

## Spine components

Vendored shadcn-style (you own the source). Each has a live preview, props table, and usage:

- Screen — status / stage / cue layout shell.
- StatusBar — glanceable top status row (time, battery), RTL logical slots.
- Readout — single-value complication with tabular numerals.
- Cue — caption / hint line; accent tone for live states.
- Button — D-pad-focusable action; emitted-light focus ring.
- GlowIcon — line-icon with a two-tier luminance rule (inert white / active green).
- List + ListRow — vertical stack of focusable rows.
- Progress — linear (native `<progress>`) or step-of-N dots.
- AsyncView — placeholder → loading → success / error renderer.

## Links

- Docs / getting started: https://ui.glasskit.app/docs
- Components reference: https://ui.glasskit.app/docs/components
- Playground (live customizer): https://ui.glasskit.app/playground
- Source: https://github.com/GlassKitApp/glasskit-ui
- Design spec (Apple-feel additive lens): https://ui.glasskit.app

## Notes

- React 19 is a peer dependency. ESM only.
- The repo is not yet public and nothing is published to npm — these are the intended URLs.
